stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results on May 6, 2026: consolidated revenue Rs 4,615 cr (+7% YoY), EBITDA Rs 1,177 cr (+13.5%), PAT Rs 582 cr (+10.6%). FY26 PAT surged 46.7% to Rs 1,835 cr. Stock jumped ~8% post results. Board approved Rs 2,300 cr refrigerant capacity expansion, but indefinitely deferred Rs 490 cr BOPP film project at Indore citing changing market conditions.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals sector facing mixed near-term outlook with H1 FY26 muted due to US tariffs on R32/R125, methyl methacrylate, and 2,4-D exports. Geopolitical issues impacting Middle East exports. However, fluorochemicals/HFC demand remains strong domestically. India chemical production projected +10.9% in 2026.
stocks.peer_comparison: SRF outperformed sector and broader Sensex on May 6 with high institutional trading volumes. Among fluorochemicals peers (Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals, Aarti Industries, Atul) all face similar US tariff headwinds, but SRF's HFC franchise and refrigerant capex commitment differentiate it.
red_flags: MarketsMojo downgraded SRF to 'Sell' from 'Hold' on 16 Feb 2026 (Mojo Score 48.0)
US tariff exposure on key fluorochemicals (R32/R125) impacts export economics
Management flagged challenging quarter due to geopolitical issues affecting Middle East exports
BOPP film project deferral signals capital allocation uncertainty
catalysts: Rs 2,300 cr refrigerant plant expansion (doubled investment) provides multi-year growth visibility
FY26 PAT +46.7% demonstrates earnings cycle recovery

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results May 6: revenue Rs 4,450 cr (+29.2% YoY), EBIT margin 14.4%, net profit Rs 612.3 cr vs Rs 261.2 cr YoY (multifold jump aided by one-time tax benefit). Stock surged 10%+ on May 6, up ~18% in 3 sessions from Rs 1,152. Cigniti merger fixed record date May 16, 2026 with 1:1 share exchange ratio. Order book at $1.75B for next 12 months.
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT sector recovery expected by FY27 driven by AI ramp-up. Midcap IT (Coforge, Persistent, Mphasis, Hexaware) outperforming large-caps with double-digit gains. Nomura, Motilal Oswal, and Choice all flag Coforge as preferred midcap IT pick. Q3/Q4 still saw furlough impact and soft macro.
stocks.peer_comparison: Coforge's 29.2% revenue growth is materially higher than large-cap IT peers. Mid-cap IT cohort (Persistent, KPIT, Mphasis) outperforming large-caps. Coforge's order book strength and Cigniti integration give it superior growth visibility.
red_flags: Q4 PAT jump significantly aided by one-time tax benefit (lower quality earnings)
Some sources flagged EPS miss versus estimates despite headline beat
Cigniti merger creates near-term share dilution complexity (1:1 swap on May 16 record date)
catalysts: $1.75B order book provides strong FY27 revenue visibility
AI-led short-cycle deal momentum starting mid-2026

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: revenue Rs 3,428 cr (+12.5% YoY), consolidated PAT Rs 244 cr (+18%). FY26 revenue Rs 10,694 cr (+3%), PAT Rs 720 cr (+15%). Stock hit new high Rs 4,550, surged 9% intraday and is up 38% in past month vs flat Sensex. Special dividend Rs 6 + final Rs 14 = Rs 20/share total (60th anniversary). Q4FY26 earnings call scheduled May 8, 2026 (today). TBWES subsidiary won Rs 1,600 cr boiler package order for ultra-supercritical thermal plant in Central India.
stocks.sector_context: BSE Capital Goods index at record highs, +17% in past two months. Union Budget 2026-27 allocated record $133.1B for infrastructure. Strong order books across power, metro, railways, T&D, data centres. China+1 tailwinds. Sector capex executing well but valuations under scrutiny.
stocks.peer_comparison: Capital goods stocks BHEL, Polycab, Thermax all rallying with index at new highs. Thermax's 38% one-month outperformance is among the strongest in capital goods cohort. Earnings growth forecast ~17.4% annually with revenue +12.6%.
red_flags: Capital Goods index valuations 'under scrutiny' per analyst commentary
Motilal Oswal maintained Sell rating (Feb 2025) citing FGD project cost overruns and margin pressure
Stock up 38% in 1 month creates short-term overbought risk and earnings-day volatility
catalysts: Q4 FY26 earnings concall today, May 8 — guidance and order book commentary key
Rs 1,600 cr TBWES boiler order anchors FY27 visibility
Kotak Institutional Equities Buy rating with TP Rs 3,575 (Dec 2025)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results May 6: consolidated revenue Rs 8,864 cr (+26.9% YoY) — highest-ever quarterly. PAT Rs 773 cr (+6.3%) — highest-ever single quarter. FY26 revenue Rs 28,884 cr (+28.9%), PAT Rs 2,672 cr (+32.3%). Stock surged 6%+ on May 7 to new 52-week high Rs 8,940. Wires & Cables segment +30% YoY, FMEG +47% YoY (solar leading), EPC -15% YoY due to project timing. Board declared Rs 47/share dividend — highest in listed history (27.2% payout). Domestic organised W&C market share up to 30-31% (from 26-27%).
stocks.sector_context: Capital goods/electrical sector at record highs with BSE Capital Goods index +17% in 2 months. Tailwinds from infrastructure capex, T&D investment, data centre buildout, solar EPC, and government infra spend. Industry-wide order book strength.
stocks.peer_comparison: Polycab's 30% W&C growth and 3-4 ppt market share gain in a single year is exceptional vs peers (Havells, KEI, Finolex). MOFSL and ICICI Securities raised target prices to Rs 9,800-10,500 implying ~10-16% upside. Stands out for breadth across W&C, FMEG-solar, and brand pricing power.
red_flags: PAT growth (+6.3%) materially lagged revenue growth (+26.9%) — possible margin compression in Q4
Stock at all-time high may face profit-booking despite strong fundamentals
catalysts: Brokerage upgrades — MOFSL/ICICI Sec TP raised to Rs 9,800-10,500
Continued market share consolidation in organised W&C segment
Solar FMEG sub-category emerging as largest growth driver

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: consolidated PAT Rs 584.2 cr (+36.6% YoY vs Rs 427.5 cr), revenue Rs 3,583 cr (+14-15.3%), EBITDA Rs 830 cr at 23.25% margin (vs 20.15% YoY) — meaningful margin expansion. Broad-based volume growth across Consumer & Bazaar and B2B segments. Board recommended Rs 11.5/share dividend. Company disclosed scheduled investor meet with Seafarer Capital on May 13, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Adhesives/construction chemicals more linked to consumer and housing/real estate cycle than commodity specialty chemicals. Less directly impacted by US tariff overhang affecting fluorochemicals peers. Domestic demand recovery and benign raw material prices supportive. Pidilite is sector leader in adhesives (Fevicol), waterproofing (Dr. Fixit).
stocks.peer_comparison: Pidilite's 36.6% PAT growth and 310 bps EBITDA margin expansion stands out vs broader specialty chemicals cohort which faced muted H1 FY26. Distinct from export-oriented chemical peers like SRF/Aarti due to domestic consumer-facing model.
red_flags: Pre-results analysts trimmed fair value estimate from ~Rs 1,619 to ~Rs 1,550 on revised growth/margin assumptions
Stock trading at premium consumer-staples-like multiples leaves limited margin for disappointment
catalysts: Strong Q4 margin expansion (310 bps) and broad-based volume growth signal pricing power
Investor meet with Seafarer Capital on May 13, 2026
